title: local_postgresql_environment_connection
description: Verify that the Spring Boot application connects successfully to the local PostgreSQL 16 instance started via docker-compose.postgres.yml using the local profile with correct database and credentials.

title: local_postgresql_environment_failure_handling
description: Simulate PostgreSQL startup failure due to port conflict or container issue and verify that the failure is clearly logged and the developer is informed to fix the environment before retrying.

title: local_admin_seeder_creation
description: Start the application with spring.profiles.active=local and verify that the seeder creates the ADMIN role if missing and creates the default admin user with correct properties without duplication on restart.

title: local_admin_seeder_skip_existing
description: Start the application with spring.profiles.active=local and verify that if the ADMIN role and admin user already exist, the seeder skips creation and no duplicate records are created.

title: local_admin_seeder_no_run_in_production
description: Start the application with spring.profiles.active=prod and verify that the local admin seeder does not run and no default admin account is created, ensuring production data remains unchanged.

title: feed_timeline_query_excludes_soft_deleted_users
description: As an authenticated user, call GET /posts/** and verify that posts from authors with deleted=true are excluded from the feed or timeline results.

title: feed_timeline_query_respects_visibility_rules
description: As an authenticated user, verify that PUBLIC posts are shown, FOLLOWERS_ONLY posts are shown only to followers or the author, and PRIVATE posts are hidden from third parties in GET /posts/** responses.

title: feed_timeline_query_non_follower_access
description: As an authenticated user who is not a follower, verify that FOLLOWERS_ONLY and PRIVATE posts from non-followed authors are omitted and only allowed PUBLIC content is returned in GET /posts/**.

title: feed_timeline_query_excludes_soft_deleted_post_owners
description: Verify that GET /posts/** excludes posts or reposts belonging to soft-deleted users by evaluating the deleted flag correctly, ensuring feed remains free of deleted-user content.

title: public_post_detail_access_control
description: Verify that GET /posts/public/{postId} returns 404 or access-denied response when the post is owned by a deleted author or is a private post, preventing exposure of sensitive content.
